title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California's housing crisis has become a humanitarian crisis.",
    "The 2022 Statewide Housing Plan estimates California needs approximately 2.5 million new units of housing over the next eight years.",
    "The state will need 180,000 new units of housing each year just to keep up with existing demand.",
    "California averages less than 100,000 new housing units per year.",
    "The state has never produced more than 20,000 new affordable homes in any year.",
    "AB 2011 would allow the state to address its affordable housing, jobs, and climate crises simultaneously.",
    "There is potential for the production of three million new housing units in just the Bay Area and Los Angeles County.",
    "The bill includes new homeownership opportunities for middle-income Californians, promoting climate-friendly development.",
    "AB 2011's housing provisions have been endorsed by both affordable housing and labor groups.",
    "Prevailing wage is required on all projects under AB 2011.",
    "For projects of 50 or more units, contractors must either participate in a state-approved apprenticeship program or request apprentices from such a program.",
    "The bill includes new enforcement mechanisms to ensure payroll and benefits requirements are met.",
    "AB 2011 will create tens of thousands of steady, well-paying jobs with health benefits."
  ]
}
```
